findings.finding: This central claim maps only to source-nist-dlmf-log, whose title, URL, and locator identify DLMF §4.2 and specifically §4.2(i). That locator does not supply the product, quotient, and power identities asserted and assessed by the lesson. General-base formulas occur in [DLMF §4.2(ii)](https://dlmf.nist.gov/4.2), while the product and quotient identities occur in [DLMF §4.8(i)](https://dlmf.nist.gov/4.8). The exact claim-to-locator evidence chain is therefore incomplete even though the elementary real-positive identities themselves are correct.
findings.resolution: Map claim-log-laws to an authoritative record with exact locators for the real-positive product, quotient, and power laws, or revise source-nist-dlmf-log so its title, URL, and locator accurately cover the supporting DLMF sections and equations.

findings.finding: The growth and decay conditions for Q(t)=Q0a^t and Q(t)=Q0e^(kt), together with the equivalence a=e^k, are material quantitative claims central to objective-interpret-change. None of the three records in claims.json states or supports these relationships. The same omission affects the half-life model used in content/060-transfer.md and defined at glossary.json#term-half-life. The source notes name only logarithm inversion, logarithm laws, and dimensionless ratios, leaving the exponential-change model untraced.
findings.resolution: Add precisely scoped claim records and authoritative support for the discrete-factor, continuous-rate, and half-life relationships, then map them through the source notes in every scene where those relationships are taught or applied.

findings.finding: The objective promises interpretation of growth factors, decay factors, doubling times, half-lives, and the distinction between discrete and continuous parameterizations. Its mapped mastery items, assessment.json#item-half-life and assessment.json#item-transfer-decay, assess only half-life and continuous exponential decay. They do not assess doubling time, growth-factor interpretation, or the discrete-versus-continuous parameterization distinction, so the mastery gate cannot establish the full declared objective.
findings.resolution: Either narrow objective-interpret-change to the performance actually assessed or add correctly keyed item and rubric evidence for the omitted growth-factor, doubling-time, and parameterization components.

findings.finding: The exact result is ln(7)/0.4 = 4.8647753726..., which rounds to 4.865 at the requested three decimal places. The acceptable-response entry also accepts 4.864, contradicting both the prompt's precision instruction and rubric-solve-exponential, which specifies approximately 4.865.
findings.resolution: Accept 4.865 for standard three-decimal rounding, or state an explicit numerical tolerance and align the prompt, acceptable responses, and rubric.

findings.finding: Independent recalculation gives ln(3.125)/ln(1.18) = 6.8841987059..., and substitution returns 1000 within numerical precision. The stated bracket between five and ten intervals and the distinction between continuous threshold time and completed intervals are correct.

limitations: The assigned scope prohibited reading site/, so the graph-node record and cross-lesson terminology ledger were not inspected. Outcome fit was instead checked against lesson.json and QRS-102 in course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md; cross-lesson terminology collisions remain unverified.
The cited public NIST pages were reachable and independently checked; no human-only or otherwise inaccessible source prevented a verdict.
Repository/schema validation was not run because this was a read-only academic review and the assigned scope excluded scripts/ and site/. This verdict makes no structural-validation claim.
